overall_verdict: The test-draft is a strong, disciplined execution of the deep-pass memo, not a quiet-loss sleeper draft. It hit the memo's recommended ~8,500-9,000-word landing almost exactly (13,431 → 9,103 words, a 32% cut) and the cut profile matches the memo's prescriptions closely: all four naming-defense thesaurus passages gone (C-1 through C-5), the DAC dump relocated to Method 1 (C-8), the SCC optimism coda dropped (C-11), the per-case re-walks de-duplicated (C-12), the Ostrom distinction collapsed from 3x to 1x (C-13), and the IPG reconciliation thicket compressed (C-10). The spine the memo identified as 'excellent and should survive intact' DID survive intact and is materially more authoritative without the defensive over-explanation. Better still, the draft delivered four of the memo's flagged value-ADDS that were not mere cuts: it inserted the Polanyi/Fraser/Harvey structural-commons grounding (the memo's #1 exposed flank), the Pistor financialization engagement (the #2 exposed flank), the Coase rebuttal (a thin flank), and it FIXED the §8 figure-drift by reconciling carbon/total to the canonical $496/$510/$504-518 set — a correctness win, not just a length win. It also fixed the buried-climax problem (I-17) by promoting the subsidy thesis to the convergence climax. So on the 'does it address everything needed to land' axis: yes, comprehensively. On the adversarial 'did it lose NONE of the value' axis the answer is: it lost a SMALL amount of genuinely load-bearing content beyond what the memo authorized — most importantly (1) the bond-forward-posting-architecture link in the externality-tail passage, and (2) the two named Commons Inversion Test sub-forms (Commons-Absence / Commons-Consumption), which are the operational heart of the discovery method the chapter claims as a core contribution and which now have no home in-chapter after the Ten-Categories and Four-Gates compressions. These are restorable in 2-4 sentences total. Secondary minor losses (Sen 'how-to-value', the reverse-direction precedent roster, the 555x prose figure) are defensible and arguably belong to other chapters. Two operational caveats: the draft still carries six [CITATION GAP] and two [RELOCATE] bracketed flags that are working scaffolding and must be resolved/stripped before any move toward render (they would trip the Stage-1a scaffolding invariant scan); and the canonical-figure fix depends on Ch 8 actually carrying $496/$510 (memo §10 dependency flag — verify before finalizing).

overall_verdict: The test-draft is a strong, faithful execution of the deep-pass memo's three priority actions: (1) it FIXES the HIGH-priority figure drift correctly ($510 total / $496 carbon, era-consistent $4.50 mine-mouth pairing); (2) it FILLS the ratified Polanyi/Fraser re-embedding gap AND the Restitution/Darity backward-half via a genuinely high-value new section that simultaneously closes three previously-EXPOSED objections (financialization/Pistor-bonds, commodify-labor, coercion); (3) it surfaces the four-step spine by subordinating the apparatus. The SAFE cuts (Clarion-Clipperton, rare-earth catalogue, renewable-commons excursus, Desmond/Susskind roll-call) lose no argument. The claim that it 'lost NONE of the value' is, however, too strong on an adversarial read for two reasons. FIRST and most important: roughly 3,600 words of genuinely valuable material (the 2008-vs-Sweden case, the eleven-cent healthcare section, the Chattanooga/Mondragon/Vienna trio, the Christophers coda) are not deleted but RELOCATED via inline brackets to chapters that have NOT yet been confirmed to receive them — the deep-pass itself flags 'confirm those chapters do not already carry the same material,' and the macro-chapter destination for the trio is left undefined. As a corpus, that value is currently in limbo: excised from Ch9, not yet landed elsewhere. A value-retention audit cannot certify 'no loss' on relocated-but-not-landed content. SECOND: the Nordic demographic-homogeneity steelman (the original's self-described strongest objection-rebuttal, with three specific pressure-tests) is compressed to assertion, a real thinning for adversarial Tier-1 readers. The draft is therefore an excellent redraft BASE, but treating it as the final chapter requires (a) confirming/executing all four relocations into live destination chapters, (b) resolving every [CITATION GAP]/[RELOCATE] scaffolding marker (which the corpus-invariant scan would block), (c) a fact-check pass on the new Polanyi/Fraser/Darity characterizations, and (d) an author decision on whether to restore the Nordic demographic rebuttal and the Public Choice think-tank courtship.

overall_verdict: The test-draft is NOT the draft the deep-pass memo specified, and it is NOT submission-clean. Two problems dominate. (1) SUBMISSION-BLOCKING SCAFFOLDING: the draft carries eight inline '[CITATION GAP: ...]' brackets plus one '[RELOCATE TO Ch9: ...]' editorial instruction embedded in the prose. These are HIGH-severity scaffolding-scan violations that were never in the original and must be stripped before any merge or submission; the 'lose NONE of the value' claim fails immediately on the fact that the draft is dirtier than the original it came from. (2) IT IS NEARLY FLAT, NOT TRIMMED: 5,434 -> 5,373 words (a 1.1% reduction) against the memo's recommended ~4,700w (~13%). The memo's two heaviest SAFE cuts -- the etymology paragraph (#1) and the black-lung fold (#6) and the rents/routing clause (#5) -- were taken cleanly, but §The Numbers anaphora (#3) and the pride-restatement consolidation (#8) were barely touched, and the two ADDs (Coase expansion ~90w + Fraser/Polanyi ~180w) added back roughly what the real cuts removed. The author asked whether the sleeper draft 'lost NONE of the value' -- on pure prose content the answer is close to yes (only two minor unflagged sentence-level losses at the Purdue close), and the two ADDs are genuine improvements the memo itself requested. But the draft also took the one TRADE cut the memo told it to HOLD (Purdue chronology #7) despite no length pressure, slightly weakening falsifiability armor against the contradictory advice. Net: low actual prose-value loss, but the draft is not safe to ship as-is because of the embedded markers, and it did not achieve the memo's tightening objective.

overall_verdict: This is a strong, disciplined revision that is closer to a NET GAIN than a quiet loss -- but it is explicitly a pre-cleanup working draft, not a ship-ready chapter, and it carries two genuine restoration items plus two conditional ones. On the cut side: every SAFE cut the deep-pass identified (C-1 abundance-masking lexicography, C-4 front-matter dedup, C-5 pattern re-mapping, C-6 capstone collapse, C-7 commute-mechanism recitation, C-8 ending dedup, plus C-9/C-10 internal compressions) was executed faithfully and the load-bearing distinctions survive in compressed form. Landing-points L (falsifiability, test-draft 195) and M (boundary/Europa, 93) -- the chapter's signature deliverables the deep-pass said to PROTECT -- are fully intact. On the add side the draft is genuinely impressive: it closes nearly every named-theorist positioning gap the deep-pass §6 flagged (Hotelling, Pigou, Coase, Parfit, Rawls, Daly, Weitzman, Sandel, Simon-Ehrlich, Pistor) at the exact points of contact, closes the ratified book-level Polanyi/Fraser gap, AND repairs two objection seams the deep-pass §7 flagged as EXPOSED/risky (the commute-lease Sandelian commodification inconsistency via the new 'disclosure not valuation' paragraph at 101, and the coercion-flattening worth-deferral caveat at 123). That is exactly the work the lens called for. The honest concerns: (1) the delivery-cost note was deleted with zero residue where the deep-pass wanted one sentence -- a small but clean lost-value item; (2) the across-time specifics and the commute arithmetic are currently thinner than the original, pending citation cleanup; (3) the draft is studded with [CITATION GAP] and [RELOCATE] brackets, so it is categorically not submission-eligible until those are resolved (no-invented-factual-claims hard rule); and (4) two compressions (immune-response, existential-gap payoff) now hard-depend on Ch 9 delivery that cannot be verified from Ch 7 alone. None of these is fatal; all are addressable by restoration rather than restart.

overall_verdict: This is NOT a true 'lost none of the value' one-shot. It is a strong, largely faithful execution of the deep-pass that nonetheless made FOUR quiet over-cuts beyond what the memo authorized, plus introduced one unverified new figure. The adds are excellent and almost all earn their place: the Polanyi/Fraser section, the Pigou naming + Pigouvian-floor reconciliation, the falsification surface, the Weitzman clause, and the Hotelling identity each close a specific deep-pass-identified gap and materially strengthen the chapter's objection-survival and lit-positioning — these are net gains, not bloat. The figure-drift fixes ($4.50, across-the-1980s, 122x) are correct and ledger-compliant. BUT the skeptic's job surfaces real quiet losses the author would not want to ship: (1) the Lewis/UMW paragraph was cut to ZERO when the memo said cut to ONE sentence — the chapter loses its only narrated political-capture demonstration; (2) the Dunbar couplet lost its load-bearing 'this debt we pay to human guile' line, which is the verbal hinge of the mask-as-priced-debt move; (3) the 13-year life-expectancy gap is now a hard number stated nowhere (two separate cuts conspired to erase it); (4) the 2008 beat exists only inside a relocation bracket, so the running prose has no macro-financial-crisis instance. Additionally, the new '~50x triangulated central estimate' is a keystone-adjacent figure NOT confirmed in the deep-pass §8 ledger audit — a drift risk that must be verified before ship. The housing/YIMBY and Public-Choice relocations are defensible AS CH8 CUTS but leave two objections unsurvived within the chapter, contingent on Ch9 actually delivering — must verify. The inline [CITATION GAP]/[RELOCATE] brackets are correct test-draft scaffolding but are hard-block invariant-gate patterns that must be stripped before any merge/render.
